details: Probing whether Reg A (moreh_softmax) and Reg C (moreh_adam) share moreh.sum-broken-on-1D root cause with Reg B

Neither moreh_softmax_backward nor moreh_adam host code calls moreh.sum. Failing test shapes are all 4D (softmax: [1,15,32,32], [10,20,96,160], [1,1,10,15]; adam: [32,32], [2,2,2,2,2,2,64,64]). Probes will assert moreh.sum is CORRECT for these shapes -> root cause of Reg A and Reg C is NOT the moreh.sum 1D bug.

1D control: shapes [5] and [32] return -inf (Reg B repro confirmed). 4D Reg A failing shapes (11 total: [15,109,64,64], [32,32], [3,32,160], [5,6,32,32], [10,20,96,160], [3,160,32], [2,3,128,160], [1,1,10,15], [1,1,10,74], [1,1,15,10], [1,1,74,32]): ALL 11 OK on moreh.sum. Verdict: Reg A does NOT share Reg B root cause.
